title: The Mabinogion: Pwyll Prince of Dyfed
source: White Book of Rhydderch; Red Book of Hergest
description: The first branch of the Mabinogi: Pwyll's journey to the Otherworld and his encounter with Rhiannon.
introduction: The Mabinogion preserves the oldest prose literature in Britain — tales of enchantment, sovereignty, and the Otherworld rooted in pre-Christian Celtic mythology. The First Branch follows Pwyll into Annwn (the Otherworld) and introduces Rhiannon, a figure widely identified with the horse-goddess Epona. These tales were composed orally centuries before their transcription.

translation.original_text: Pwyll Pendeuic Dyuet a oed yn arglwyd ar seith cantref Dyuet.
translation.text: Pwyll Prince of Dyfed was lord over the seven cantrefs of Dyfed.

translation.original_text: Ac ual y byd yn eisted, llyma uorwyn yn dyuot ar uarch canwelw mawr.
translation.text: And as he sat, behold a maiden on a great pale-white horse coming along the road.
